1.4. Tensor representation

Dumb working definition: a compact group is a group whose elements fit into a space that is finite in extent, so you can meaningfully average over all group elements. Examples: , any finite group. Nonexamples: ,

Like with finite groups (Maschke’s theorem), we can decompose a representation into a finite direct sum:

Theorem 1 (Peter-Weyl). Let be a compact group. Every finite-dimensional continuous unitary representation of decomposes as a finite direct sum of irreducible representations:

(9)

where denotes multiplicity.

Given two representations and of , their tensor product is also a representation, with group action:
